pattern: Tracking the earliest legal day per task type lets a single forward sweep skip exactly the breaks the cooldown forces.
hints: Walk the tasks in order, keeping a running clock of the current day.
For each task type remember the earliest day it may next run, which is its last day plus space plus one.
Advance the clock to the max of itself and that earliest day, do the task there, then record day plus space plus one for that type.

edge_inputs.description: all distinct types, no cooldown ever triggers
every task same type, maximal forced waiting
repeat far enough apart that no break is needed

stress.description: many tasks drawn from a small type pool to force frequent cooldowns
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    pool = rng.randint(1, 50)
    types = rng.sample(range(1, 10**9), pool)
    tasks = [rng.choice(types) for _ in range(n)]
    space = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [tasks, space]
